chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: Is there camera pointing at the speaker or it's static?

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: The one in the back seems to be pointing at Maarten

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: There's two cameras in here, one pointing roughly at the mic line and one pointing towards the chairs and speaker (I think)

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: yes, it got stuck pointing at the chairs. Now it's good thank you

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Yes, and we try to capture who's speaking, but we missed the presenter change, so thanks Pawel for the heads up :sweat_smile:

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Feel free to nudge us if we miss it again

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: Not sure I like the registry is the parent of registrars thing.

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: well we do have registrars owning registries...

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: I guess it is just fitting things into paths.... not an actual parent/child relationship

chat.author: Barbara Bellis
chat.text: We have registries, and then registry operators that have multiple registries - so that's another level, and then the provider which is the parent of them all.

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: it's like - give me all registrars of .example TLD, or .foo TLD if ran in the same system and sharing registrar objects

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: could also use the repository idea from EPP

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: ah, so it seems as though it's a semantic mismatch between the application model and JSON arrays.... perhaps... if I understood that correctly

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: (the application data model, that is)

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: yes

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: is anyone asking for OATH in RPP environmnets

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: yes

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: this is definitely a good idea.

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: Yep I can think of a bunch of stuff I could do with this

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: (One of them is something Maarten's already done and I think will be showing off later, which is much slicker and more secure transfers)

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: Thanks Pawel. I think this adds "too many" moving parts and the costs outweigh the benefits. Which is easy for me to say since I don't run a registry.

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: @jim also we need to come up with something better than basic auth, so OAuth2 in m2m variant is the minimum we can attempt to use.

chat.author: Maxwell Gerber
chat.text: How does authentication/authorization usually work in RPP environments today?

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: Other use-cases may open up on top

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: Going to take a guess that most registries that might be interested in rpp are already configured as OAuth providers in some way

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: I feel their pain. :grinning:

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: what is painful about it?

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: You used Oauth just to be chatting here. :)

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: Complexity.

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: I run an OATH server? :-) News to me and my laptop.

chat.author: Maxwell Gerber
chat.text: What would you do instead? Basic auth or API Keys?

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: I don't know enough about the problem space to say what's best.

chat.author: Barbara Bellis
chat.text: Client cert authentication

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: Might be worthwhile running the scenario by an oauth expert.

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: Is there a reason this same pattern couldn't work for a push transfer?

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: I think what Rick is getting at is that the different models probably have different solutions.

chat.author: Gordon Dick
chat.text: It looked like the auth code still exists when the transfer process uses this model - the EPP code gets sent to the registry... it is just the EPP code is passed around more easily.

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: Rick is that an old hand?

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: I think it's down now??

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: thx

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: For real, we need more people - some IoT folks stole half our table :(

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: I hear you

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: why should we not require OAuth 2.0 ?  (for security reasons)

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: sorry I'm not there in person
